mathematical_rationale: A fooling set S for a Boolean function F : X × Y → Bool contains pairs (x_i, y_i) where F is true on the diagonal and false on the off-diagonal. If a Turing machine computes F by exchanging crossing sequences across a spatial cut, then distinct inputs on the X side must produce distinct crossing sequences; otherwise, the Y side would respond identically to a given y, contradicting the fooling set property. Formally, if C : X → CrossSeq encodes the crossing sequence, and there exists an evaluator eval_Y such that F x y = eval_Y (C x) y, then for any fooling set S, C restricted to the X-projection of S is injective. This establishes a communication lower bound: the number of distinct crossing sequences needed is at least the size of the projection.
lean_4_code_delta:
import Mathlib.Data.Finset.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Basic

variable {X Y : Type} [DecidableEq X] [DecidableEq Y] [DecidableEq (X × Y)]

def FoolingSet (F : X → Y → Bool) (S : Finset (X × Y)) : Prop :=
  (∀ p ∈ S, F p.1 p.2 = true) ∧
  (∀ p1 p2 ∈ S, p1.1 ≠ p2.1 → F p1.1 p2.2 = false ∨ F p2.1 p1.2 = false)

variable {CrossSeq : Type} (C : X → CrossSeq) (eval_Y : CrossSeq → Y → Bool)
  (h_correct : ∀ x y, F x y = eval_Y (C x) y) (F : X → Y → Bool)

lemma fooling_set_implies_injective_C_on_projection
    (S : Finset (X × Y)) (hS : FoolingSet F S) :
    Set.InjOn C (Finset.image Prod.fst S : Set X) := by
  intro x1 hx1 x2 hx2 hC_eq
  simp only [Finset.mem_coe, Finset.mem_image] at hx1 hx2
  rcases hx1 with ⟨⟨x1', y1⟩, h1, rfl⟩
  rcases hx2 with ⟨⟨x2', y2⟩, h2, rfl⟩
  by_contra h_ne
  have hF_true1 := hS.1 _ h1
  have hF_true2 := hS.1 _ h2
  have hF_false_cond := hS.2 _ h1 _ h2 h_ne
  rw [h_correct] at hF_true1 hF_true2 hF_false_cond
  rw [hC_eq] at hF_true2
  cases hF_false_cond with
  | inl h_false =>
      rw [h_correct] at h_false
      contradiction
  | inr h_false =>
      rw [h_correct] at h_false
      contradiction
expected_verifier_state: Definition `FoolingSet` added with correct diagonal/off-diagonal condition; lemma `fooling_set_implies_injective_C_on_projection` proved, establishing that the crossing sequence mapping C is injective on the X-projection of any fooling set, given the correctness of the protocol representation.
